info.name: Bifix API
info.description:
REST API for Bifix — mobile Flutter app backend and integrations.

---

## Quick start (Postman)

1. Start the API: `./vendor/bin/sail up -d`
2. Load demo data: `./vendor/bin/sail artisan migrate:fresh --seed`
3. Import `postman/Local.postman_environment.json`
4. Run **Auth → Login — Test User**
5. Run **Bikes → List My Bikes** (expect 2 bikes)
6. Run **Rides → List Rides** (My Giant has 2 demo rides)

---

## Demo data (`DemoSeeder`)

Command: `php artisan db:seed --class=DemoSeeder` or `migrate:fresh --seed`

### Users

| Email | Password | Name | Bikes |
|-------|----------|------|-------|
| `test@example.com` | `password` | Test User | 2 |
| `jane@example.com` | `password` | Jane Doe | 1 |

### Catalog (brands & models)

| Brand | Slug | Models |
|-------|------|--------|
| Giant | `giant` | Explore E+ 2, Trance X E+ 3 |
| Trek | `trek` | Domane+ SLR 6, Powerfly 5 |
| Specialized | `specialized` | Turbo Vado 4.0 |

### Battery types

| Name | Slug |
|------|------|
| Lead | `lead` |
| Lithium | `lithium` |

### Preloaded bikes

**test@example.com:**
- *My Giant* — Giant Explore E+ 2, SN-GIANT-001, 50 km (2 rides)
- *Trek road bike* — Trek Domane+ SLR 6, SN-TREK-001, 890 km

**jane@example.com:**
- *Urban Vado* — Specialized Turbo Vado 4.0, SN-SPEC-001, 45 km (1 ride)

---

## Authentication

The API uses **Laravel Sanctum** Bearer tokens.

1. Run **Login — Test User** or **Login — Jane Doe** to obtain a token.
2. The token is saved automatically to `{{token}}`.
3. All catalog, bike, and ride routes require `Authorization: Bearer {{token}}`.

## Endpoint reference

| Method | Route | Postman folder |
|--------|-------|----------------|
| GET | `/api/v1/health` | Health |
| POST | `/api/v1/auth/register` | Auth |
| POST | `/api/v1/auth/login` | Auth |
| GET | `/api/v1/auth/me` | Auth |
| POST | `/api/v1/auth/logout` | Auth |
| GET | `/api/v1/brands` | Catalog |
| GET | `/api/v1/brands/{id}` | Catalog |
| GET | `/api/v1/bike-models` | Catalog |
| GET | `/api/v1/bike-models/{id}` | Catalog |
| GET | `/api/v1/battery-types` | Catalog |
| GET | `/api/v1/bikes` | Bikes |
| POST | `/api/v1/bikes` | Bikes |
| GET | `/api/v1/bikes/{id}` | Bikes |
| PUT | `/api/v1/bikes/{id}` | Bikes |
| DELETE | `/api/v1/bikes/{id}` | Bikes |
| GET | `/api/v1/bikes/{id}/rides` | Rides |
| POST | `/api/v1/bikes/{id}/rides` | Rides |
| GET | `/api/v1/bikes/{id}/rides/{id}` | Rides |
| PUT | `/api/v1/bikes/{id}/rides/{id}` | Rides |
| DELETE | `/api/v1/bikes/{id}/rides/{id}` | Rides |

ER diagram: see `docs/er-diagram.md` in the repository.

## Flutter flow (register a bike)

```
Login → GET /brands → GET /bike-models?brand_id={id} → POST /bikes → GET /bikes
```

### Recommended Flutter integration

| Step | Endpoint | App usage |
|------|----------|-----------|
| 1 | `POST /auth/login` | Store token in `flutter_secure_storage` |
| 2 | `GET /brands` | Brand picker (cache locally) |
| 3 | `GET /bike-models?brand_id=` | Model picker filtered by brand |
| 4 | `POST /bikes` | Registration form with `bike_model_id` |
| 5 | `GET /bikes` | "My bikes" list |
| 6 | `POST /bikes/{id}/rides` | Log a ride (updates odometer) |
| 7 | `GET /bikes/{id}/rides` | Ride history |

### Common headers (Flutter / Dio)

```dart
headers: {
  'Accept': 'application/json',
  'Content-Type': 'application/json',
  'Authorization': 'Bearer $token',
}
```

### Response format

- Single resource: `{ "data": { ... } }`
- Collections: `{ "data": [ ... ] }`
- Validation errors (422): `{ "message": "...", "errors": { "field": ["..."] } }`
- Unauthenticated (401): `{ "message": "Unauthenticated." }`

## Rate limiting

Login and Register: **10 requests/minute**.

## Collection variables

| Variable | Description |
|----------|-------------|
| `token` | Sanctum token (auto-set after login) |
| `brandId` | Brand ID (auto-set after List Brands) |
| `bikeModelId` | Model ID (auto-set after List Bike Models) |
| `bikeId` | User bike ID (auto-set after List My Bikes / Create Bike) |
| `rideId` | Ride ID (auto-set after List / Create Ride) |

variable.description: Brand ID — auto-set after List Brands
Model ID — auto-set after List Bike Models
User bike ID — auto-set after List My Bikes / Create Bike
Ride ID — auto-set after List / Create Ride

item.name: Health
item.description: Service monitoring and health check endpoints.

request.description:
Verify que el servicio esté en ejecución.

**Respuesta 200:**
```json
{
  "status": "ok",
  "service": "bifix-api"
}
```

item.name: Auth
item.description:
Authentication with Laravel Sanctum (Bearer tokens).

### Demo users (after `migrate:fresh --seed`)

| Request | Email | Password | Bikes | Demo rides |
|---------|-------|----------|-------|------------|
| **Login — Test User** | `test@example.com` | `password` | 2 | 2 on My Giant (`bikeId=1`) |
| **Login — Jane Doe** | `jane@example.com` | `password` | 1 | 1 on Urban Vado |

Use **Login — Test User**, then **Bikes → List My Bikes → Rides → List Rides**.

request.description:
Register un nuevo usuario y devuelve un token de acceso.

### Body (JSON)

| Campo | Tipo | Requerido | Descripción |
|-------|------|-----------|-------------|
| `name` | string | Sí | Nombre del usuario (máx. 255) |
| `email` | string | Sí | Email único |
| `password` | string | Sí | Contraseña (mín. 8 caracteres) |
| `password_confirmation` | string | Sí | Confirmación de contraseña |
| `device_name` | string | Sí | Nombre del dispositivo/cliente |

### Respuesta 201
```json
{
  "data": {
    "user": {
      "id": 1,
      "name": "Jane Doe",
      "email": "jane@example.com",
      "email_verified_at": null,
      "created_at": "2026-06-07T00:00:00+00:00",
      "updated_at": "2026-06-07T00:00:00+00:00"
    },
    "token": "1|..."
  }
}
```

### Errores
- **422** — Validación fallida (email duplicado, contraseña débil, etc.)

response.name: Validation Error

request.description:
Autentica un usuario existente y devuelve un token de acceso.

Usa las variables `{{email}}` y `{{password}}` del entorno. Tras el seed, prueba con:
- `test@example.com` / `password` (2 bikes)
- `jane@example.com` / `password` (1 bici)

Ver también **Login — Test User** y **Login — Jane Doe** con credenciales fijas.

### Body (JSON)

| Campo | Tipo | Requerido | Descripción |
|-------|------|-----------|-------------|
| `email` | string | Sí | Email del usuario |
| `password` | string | Sí | Contraseña |
| `device_name` | string | Sí | Nombre del dispositivo/cliente |

### Respuesta 200
```json
{
  "data": {
    "user": { ... },
    "token": "2|..."
  }
}
```

### Errores
- **422** — Credenciales incorrectas
- **429** — Rate limit excedido (10 req/min)

response.name: Invalid Credentials

request.description:
Login con usuario demo del seeder.

**Credenciales:** `test@example.com` / `password`

**Datos precargados:**
- 2 bikes (*My Giant* id=1, *Trek road bike* id=2)
- 2 rides en My Giant (32 km + 18 km → odómetro 50 km)

**Flujo recomendado:**
1. Este request
2. **Bikes → List My Bikes** (guarda `bikeId`)
3. **Rides → List Rides**

Requiere: `./vendor/bin/sail artisan migrate:fresh --seed`

Login con segunda usuaria demo del seeder.

**Credenciales:** `jane@example.com` / `password`

**Datos precargados:** 1 bike (*Urban Vado* — Specialized Turbo Vado 4.0)

Útil para probar aislamiento entre usuarios: las bikes de `test@example.com` no deben ser visibles con este token.

Devuelve el perfil del usuario autenticado.

**Requiere:** `Authorization: Bearer {{token}}`

### Respuesta 200
```json
{
  "data": {
    "id": 1,
    "name": "Jane Doe",
    "email": "jane@example.com",
    "email_verified_at": null,
    "created_at": "2026-06-07T00:00:00+00:00",
    "updated_at": "2026-06-07T00:00:00+00:00"
  }
}
```

### Errores
- **401** — Token ausente, inválido o revocado

Revoca el token de acceso actual.

**Requiere:** `Authorization: Bearer {{token}}`

### Respuesta 200
```json
{
  "message": "Logged out successfully."
}
```

Tras el logout, el token queda invalidado y la variable `token` se limpia automáticamente.

### Errores
- **401** — Token ausente o inválido

item.name: Catalog
item.description:
Reference catalog (`CatalogSeeder`) for bike registration.

**Flow:** List Brands → List Bike Models (`brand_id`) → Create Bike.

All demo models use Lithium (`lithium`) battery and Bosch motor.

query.description: Filtrar modelos por marca (recomendado en Flutter)

request.description:
List modelos de bike. Filtrar por `brand_id` tras seleccionar marca.

**Flutter:** llamar con `brand_id` del paso anterior. Mostrar `name`, `range_km`, `battery_wh` en la UI.

### Query params

| Param | Tipo | Requerido | Descripción |
|-------|------|-----------|-------------|
| `brand_id` | int | No | Filtra modelos de una marca |

### Respuesta 200
```json
{
  "data": [
    {
      "id": 1,
      "name": "Explore E+ 2",
      "slug": "explore-e-2",
      "year": 2024,
      "frame_type": "city",
      "motor_brand": "Bosch",
      "battery_wh": 500,
      "range_km": 80,
      "brand": { "id": 1, "name": "Giant", "slug": "giant" },
      "battery_type": { "id": 2, "name": "Lithium", "slug": "lithium" }
    }
  ]
}
```

Detail de un modelo por ID.

**Flutter:** pantalla de confirmación antes de registrar la bici.

### Respuesta 200
```json
{
  "data": {
    "id": 1,
    "name": "Explore E+ 2",
    "slug": "explore-e-2",
    "year": 2024,
    "frame_type": "city",
    "motor_brand": "Bosch",
    "battery_wh": 500,
    "range_km": 80,
    "brand": { "id": 1, "name": "Giant", "slug": "giant" },
    "battery_type": { "id": 2, "name": "Lithium", "slug": "lithium" }
  }
}
```

item.name: Bikes
item.description:
CRUD for authenticated user's bikes.

### Demo data (`DemoSeeder`)

**Test User** (`test@example.com`):

| ID | Nickname | Model | Serial | Odometer |
|----|----------|-------|--------|----------|
| 1 | My Giant | Giant Explore E+ 2 | SN-GIANT-001 | 50 km |
| 2 | Trek road bike | Trek Domane+ SLR 6 | SN-TREK-001 | 890 km |

**Jane Doe** (`jane@example.com`):

| ID | Nickname | Model | Serial | Odometer |
|----|----------|-------|--------|----------|
| 3 | Urban Vado | Specialized Turbo Vado 4.0 | SN-SPEC-001 | 45 km |

> My Giant odometer (50 km) = sum of demo rides (32 + 18).

Bikes are scoped to the authenticated user. Other users' bikes return **404**.

request.description:
Register una bike para el usuario autenticado.

**Flutter:** enviar tras seleccionar modelo en el catálogo. Solo `bike_model_id` es obligatorio.

### Body (JSON)

| Campo | Tipo | Requerido | Descripción |
|-------|------|-----------|-------------|
| `bike_model_id` | int | **Sí** | ID del modelo (de List Bike Models) |
| `nickname` | string | No | Apodo personal (máx. 255) |
| `serial_number` | string | No | Número de serie (único) |
| `frame_number` | string | No | Número de cuadro (único) |
| `color` | string | No | Color |
| `odometer_km` | int | No | Kilometraje acumulado (≥ 0) |
| `purchased_at` | date | No | Fecha de compra (`YYYY-MM-DD`) |

### Respuesta 201
```json
{
  "data": { "id": 1, "nickname": "My e-bike", "model": { ... }, ... }
}
```

### Errores
- **422** — Validación (modelo inexistente, serial duplicado)
- **401** — No autenticado

Detail de una bike del usuario.

**Flutter:** pantalla de ficha/detalle de bici.

### Errores
- **404** — Bici no encontrada o no pertenece al usuario
- **401** — No autenticado

Update una bike del usuario. Envía solo los campos a modificar.

**Flutter:** formulario de edición. Usar `PUT` con body parcial.

### Body (JSON) — todos opcionales

| Campo | Tipo | Descripción |
|-------|------|-------------|
| `bike_model_id` | int | Cambiar modelo |
| `nickname` | string | Apodo |
| `serial_number` | string | Número de serie |
| `frame_number` | string | Número de cuadro |
| `color` | string | Color |
| `odometer_km` | int | Kilometraje |
| `purchased_at` | date | Fecha de compra |

### Respuesta 200
```json
{ "data": { ... } }
```

Delete una bike del usuario.

**Flutter:** confirmar con diálogo antes de llamar. Tras éxito, refrescar lista.

### Respuesta 204
Sin body.

### Errores
- **404** — Bici no encontrada o no pertenece al usuario

item.name: Rides
item.description:
Ride logging nested under `/bikes/{bikeId}/rides`.

### Odometer sync

Creating, updating, or deleting a ride automatically syncs `Bike.odometer_km`.

### Demo data (My Giant, `bikeId=1` after seed)

| Distance | Notes |
|----------|-------|
| 32 km | Commute to work |
| 18 km | Ride home |

request.description:
List los rides de una bike del usuario.

**Demo:** Login — Test User → List My Bikes → 2 rides en My Giant.

**Flutter:** historial de rides en ficha de bici.

### Respuesta 200 (My Giant, con seed)
```json
{
  "data": [
    {
      "id": 2,
      "bike_id": 1,
      "distance_km": 18,
      "started_at": "2026-05-30T17:30:00+00:00",
      "ended_at": "2026-05-30T18:05:00+00:00",
      "notes": "Ride home"
    },
    {
      "id": 1,
      "bike_id": 1,
      "distance_km": 32,
      "started_at": "2026-05-28T08:00:00+00:00",
      "ended_at": "2026-05-28T09:10:00+00:00",
      "notes": "Commute to work"
    }
  ]
}
```

Register un nuevo ride. **Incrementa** `odometer_km` de la bici.

**Flutter:** tras tracking GPS o entrada manual de distancia.

### Body (JSON)

| Campo | Tipo | Requerido | Descripción |
|-------|------|-----------|-------------|
| `distance_km` | int | **Sí** | Kilómetros del ride (≥ 1) |
| `started_at` | datetime | No | Inicio (ISO 8601) |
| `ended_at` | datetime | No | Fin (≥ started_at) |
| `notes` | string | No | Notas (máx. 1000) |

### Respuesta 201
```json
{
  "data": {
    "id": 3,
    "bike_id": 1,
    "distance_km": 25,
    "started_at": "2026-06-07T07:30:00+00:00",
    "ended_at": "2026-06-07T08:15:00+00:00",
    "notes": "Morning ride",
    "created_at": "2026-06-07T00:00:00+00:00",
    "updated_at": "2026-06-07T00:00:00+00:00"
  }
}
```

> Tras crear, el odómetro de la bike aumenta en `distance_km`.

Detail de un ride.

### Errores
- **404** — Ride o bike no pertenecen al usuario
Update un ride. Si cambia `distance_km`, **ajusta** el odómetro de la bici.

Delete un ride. **Reduce** `odometer_km` de la bici.

### Respuesta 204
Sin body.
